description:
You are a FinOps analyst who has been asked to produce a concise cost summary from two months of raw Google-Cloud-usage CSV logs that already reside on the workstation.  
    Everything must be done strictly from the Linux terminal, without installing third-party programs other than what is already available in a typical Debian/Ubuntu environment ( awk, grep, sort, uniq, sed, cut, etc. are all allowed ).

    1. Raw data location  
       • Folder: /home/user/cloud_logs  
       • Files present when the session starts:  
         - /home/user/cloud_logs/usage-2023-09.csv  
         - /home/user/cloud_logs/usage-2023-10.csv  

    2. CSV format of every raw log (header is always the first line)  
       resource_id,service,usage_hours,cost  

    3. Required calculations per month  
       a. The total monthly spend (sum of the “cost” column).  
       b. The spend per service ( “service” column ) summed across the month.  
       c. Identify the 3 most expensive services for the month, sorted from most to least expensive.  
       • All money values must be rounded to exactly two decimal places.  
       • Service names in the top-3 list must preserve the exact spelling found in the CSV.

    4. Expected output artefact  
       After finishing your analysis, create the single text file  
       /home/user/cloud_logs/reports/cost_summary_report.txt  

       The directory /home/user/cloud_logs/reports does not exist yet; create it as part of your work.  
       The file must have *exactly* the structure below so that an automated test script can validate it byte-for-byte (Unix line endings “\n”).  

       CLOUD COST SUMMARY REPORT  
       == September 2023 ==  
       Total Spend: $<SEPT_TOTAL>  
       Service Breakdown:  
         <Service-1>: $<cost>  
         <Service-2>: $<cost>  
         … one line per service, sorted by decreasing cost …  
       Top 3 Services: <Service-A>, <Service-B>, <Service-C>  

       == October 2023 ==  
       Total Spend: $<OCT_TOTAL>  
       Service Breakdown:  
         <Service-1>: $<cost>  
         <Service-2>: $<cost>  
         …  
       Top 3 Services: <Service-A>, <Service-B>, <Service-C>  

       (Blank line after each “Top 3 Services” line is optional.)  
       The test harness will do a strict string comparison, including two spaces before every service line.

    5. Additional requirements  
       • Do *not* modify, delete or rename the original CSV files.  
       • All calculations must be derived from the raw logs; do not hard-code the answers.  
       • Display intermediate console output if it helps you, but only the final file is graded.  

    6. Verification hint  
       After you think you are done, display the contents of /home/user/cloud_logs/reports/cost_summary_report.txt to the console so you can self-check the spelling, ordering, indentation and the number formatting established in step 4.
truth:
<!--
    Pre-existing log file #1
    -->
    <file path="/home/user/cloud_logs/usage-2023-09.csv"><![CDATA[
resource_id,service,usage_hours,cost
r1,Compute Engine,720,96.00
r2,Cloud Storage,5000,25.00
r3,BigQuery,100,150.00
r4,Compute Engine,100,13.33
r5,Cloud SQL,200,90.00
r6,Cloud Storage,3000,15.00
]]></file>

    <!--
    Pre-existing log file #2
    -->
    <file path="/home/user/cloud_logs/usage-2023-10.csv"><![CDATA[
resource_id,service,usage_hours,cost
r1,Compute Engine,744,99.20
r2,Cloud Storage,5500,27.50
r3,BigQuery,90,135.00
r4,Compute Engine,120,16.00
r7,Cloud Functions,1000000,5.00
r6,Cloud Storage,3500,17.50
]]></file>

    <!--
    File that the agent must create
    -->
    <expected_output path="/home/user/cloud_logs/reports/cost_summary_report.txt"><![CDATA[
CLOUD COST SUMMARY REPORT
== September 2023 ==
Total Spend: $389.33
Service Breakdown:
  BigQuery: $150.00
  Compute Engine: $109.33
  Cloud SQL: $90.00
  Cloud Storage: $40.00
Top 3 Services: BigQuery, Compute Engine, Cloud SQL

== October 2023 ==
Total Spend: $300.20
Service Breakdown:
  BigQuery: $135.00
  Compute Engine: $115.20
  Cloud Storage: $45.00
  Cloud Functions: $5.00
Top 3 Services: BigQuery, Compute Engine, Cloud Storage
]]></expected_output>
